Interface VitalSignsMonitor

All Superinterfaces:
AutoCloseable, Device, Sensor<VitalSigns>
All Known Implementing Classes:
SimulatedVitalSignsMonitor
public interface VitalSignsMonitor extends Sensor<VitalSigns>
Interface for vital signs monitoring devices (e.g., bedside monitors).

Provides streamable access to real-time vital signs data and associated waveforms.

* @see VitalSigns
Since:
1.0
Author:
Silvere Martin-Michiellot, Gemini AI (Google DeepMind)

  • Method Summary

    Modifier and Type
    Method
    Description
    int
    getChannelCount()
    Gets the number of data channels provided by this monitor.
    double[]
    getECGWaveform()
    Gets the ECG (Electrocardiogram) waveform data.
    double[]
    getPlethWaveform()
    Gets the SpO2 (Plethysmograph) waveform data.
    double
    getSampleRate()
    Gets the sample rate for waveforms.
    default VitalSigns
    getVitalSigns()
    Gets the current vital signs readings.
    boolean
    isAlarming()
    Checks if the monitor is currently alarming.

    close

    readValue

  • Method Details

    • getVitalSigns

      default VitalSigns getVitalSigns()
      Gets the current vital signs readings. Shortcut for readValue().
      Returns:
      current vital signs

    • getECGWaveform

      double[] getECGWaveform()
      Gets the ECG (Electrocardiogram) waveform data.
      Returns:
      array of ECG amplitude values

    • getPlethWaveform

      double[] getPlethWaveform()
      Gets the SpO2 (Plethysmograph) waveform data.
      Returns:
      array of pleth amplitude values

    • getSampleRate

      double getSampleRate()
      Gets the sample rate for waveforms.
      Returns:
      samples per second in Hz

    • getChannelCount

      int getChannelCount()
      Gets the number of data channels provided by this monitor.
      Returns:
      channel count

    • isAlarming

      boolean isAlarming()
      Checks if the monitor is currently alarming.
      Returns:
      true if an alert condition is active
